notes: This pass again used the upgraded flow: tiny-veneer detection first, then caller-quality checks on the surviving targets inside 1A60..1AEF.

C3:1A60 and C3:1A7B both have same-bank raw callers from already closed low-bank C3 code (C3:0CAA and C3:0232 respectively), but the local byte structure before 1AD6 still does not stabilize into a trustworthy owner/helper split, so that lead-in is frozen as mixed opcode-looking content rather than promoted prematurely.
C3:1AD6..1ADA is a confirmed veneer: JSL $C30D0E ; RTS. The target lies inside the already closed C3:0D0D..0D5B owner, making this wrapper structurally stronger than the surrounding bytes.
C3:1ADB..1AEF shifts immediately into table-like inline data after the wrapper, so the next live seam is reduced to 1AF0, whose RTL byte remains only a weak candidate until caller context improves.
